commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The branch needs author repair plus maintainer security/product review and after-fix real behavior proof, so it is not a safe automated repair lane despite the narrow code defect.; Needs attention: The patch introduces plaintext persistence of live login cookies and currently can resurrect stale cookies after a successful empty snapshot.; Review finding: [P1] Clear the sidecar when CDP returns no cookies — ex... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds managed Chrome cookie save/restore through CDP, periodic and stop-time snapshots, focused tests, and CLI docs for a new cookie sidecar.

PR surface: Source +136, Tests +167, Docs +4. Total +307 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced defect: the source shows a successful empty Storage.getCookies result returns without clearing the existing sidecar, and restoreManagedChromeCookies later reads that stale file. The original bug also has a linked current-main live repro, but I did not rerun Chrome locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs author repair plus maintainer security/product review and after-fix real behavior proof, so it is not a safe automated repair lane despite the narrow code defect.
